findings.title: Need for Notification Priority Levels
findings.description: Users require a system to differentiate between critical and routine notifications to avoid missing important messages. A three-tier priority system is suggested to visually indicate urgency.
assumptions: Users are overwhelmed by the current notification system.
Differentiating notification urgency will improve user experience.

evidence.excerpt: Users need a way to distinguish between critical alerts and routine updates.
Sarah described the experience as 'drowning in noise'... transactional alerts carry the same visual weight as critical incidents.

recommendations: Implement a three-tier notification priority system.

findings.title: Desire for Intelligent Notification Filtering
findings.description: Power users express a willingness to pay for a tier that offers intelligent filtering of notifications, indicating a strong demand for personalized notification management.
assumptions: Users are willing to invest more for a better notification experience.

evidence.excerpt: She would pay more for a tier that offers intelligent filtering.

recommendations: Explore premium tier options with intelligent filtering features.

findings.title: Need for Notification Batching
findings.description: Users prefer receiving low-priority notifications in a batch format rather than individually, which would help reduce notification fatigue.
assumptions: Batching notifications will enhance user engagement.

evidence.excerpt: Implement ML-based notification batching... batch low-priority notifications into a digest.
Marcus wants digest-style summaries instead of individual pings for non-urgent updates.

recommendations: Develop and implement a notification batching feature.

findings.title: Granular Notification Preferences Needed
findings.description: Admins require the ability to set granular notification preferences for teams and individuals, indicating a need for more customizable notification settings.
assumptions: Customizable settings will improve user satisfaction and compliance.

evidence.excerpt: Users should be able to opt in/out of specific notification categories.
Priya's top request is granular notification preferences.

recommendations: Create a user preference page for notification categories.

findings.title: Accessibility Issues with Notification Center
findings.description: Several users have reported accessibility challenges, particularly for those using screen readers and colorblind individuals, indicating a need for improved design.
assumptions: Improving accessibility will enhance user experience for diverse user groups.

evidence.excerpt: Several team members using screen readers have difficulty navigating the notification center.

recommendations: Conduct an accessibility audit and redesign the notification center.

summary: The analysis reveals a strong need for differentiated notification prioritization and intelligent filtering to reduce user fatigue. Additionally, users express a desire for customizable settings and improved accessibility features, indicating significant areas for product enhancement.
